node.title: Uber | Phone Screen | Merge Sorted Interval Lists

topic.title: Uber | Phone Screen | Merge Sorted Interval Lists

post.content:
The question was to merge two separate lists of intervals. The list of individual intervals were sorted. 

My approach was as below:

1. Merge the [two sorted lists](https://leetcode.com/problems/merge-two-sorted-lists) in a single sorted list.
2. Implementing the merging of intersecting intervals.

The leetcode problem can be found [here](https://leetcode.com/problems/merge-intervals/). 

Wish me luck for the onsite rounds.

You can do this in O(N) time and O(N). Since the intervals are sorted you can iterate over both lists with separate indexes and then add the interval with the lowest start time. After you combine the lists you can run a normal merge interval pattern without sorting. O(n) space complexity comes from creating an new array to combine the two intervals.

```
Input: 
intervalsA = [[1,3],[2,6],[8,10],[15,18]]
intervalsB = [[0,1], [9, 11], [20, 26]
Output: [[0,6],[8,11],[15,18], [20, 26]]
```

```
def mergeSortedIntervals(intervalsA, intervalsB):
    if len(intervalsA) == 0:
        return intervalsB
    elif len(intervalsB) == 0:
       return intervalsA

    combinedIntervals = combineSortedIntervals(intervalsA, intervalsB)

    merged = [combinedIntervals[0]]
    lastInterval = combinedIntervals[0]
    
    for i in range(1, len(combinedIntervals)):
        currInterval = combinedIntervals[i]
        if currInterval[0] <= lastInterval[1]: 
            lastInterval[0] = min(currInterval[0], lastInterval[0])
            lastInterval[1] = max(currInterval[1], lastInterval[1])
            merged[-1] = lastInterval
        else:
            lastInterval = currInterval
            merged.append(lastInterval)
    return merged

def combineSortedIntervals(arrA, arrB):
    i, j = 0, 0
    combined = []
    while i < len(arrA) and j < len(arrB):
        intervalA, intervalB = arrA[i], arrB[j]
        if intervalA[0] < intervalB[0]:
            combined.append(intervalA)
            i += 1
        else:
            combined.append(intervalB)
            j += 1

    while i < len(arrA):
         combined.append(arrA[i])
         i += 1

    while j < len(arrB):
        combined.append(arrB[j])
        j += 1 
    return combined
```
Can you share some sample inputs and output
which location ?  and how many years of experience? \n
was sort function allowed or you have to implement it on your own?
